event: Copernicus publishes De revolutionibus orbium coelestium
William Gilbert publishes De Magnete
Johannes Kepler publishes Astronomia nova
Galileo Galilei publishes Sidereus Nuncius
William Harvey publishes Exercitatio Anatomica de Motu Cordis et Sanguinis in Animalibus
Galileo publishes Dialogue Concerning the Two Chief World Systems
Robert Boyle formulates Boyle's law
Robert Hooke publishes Micrographia
Ole Rømer makes the first quantitative measurement of the speed of light
Isaac Newton publishes Philosophiæ Naturalis Principia Mathematica
Carl Linnaeus publishes Systema Naturae
Benjamin Franklin conducts his kite experiment
Mikhail Lomonosov discovers the atmosphere of Venus
Joseph Priestley discovers oxygen
William Herschel discovers Uranus
Charles-Augustin de Coulomb introduces Coulomb's law
Antoine Lavoisier publishes Elements of Chemistry
Edward Jenner develops the smallpox vaccine
Alessandro Volta invents the voltaic pile
Thomas Young conducts the double-slit experiment
John Dalton proposes his atomic theory
Amedeo Avogadro proposes Avogadro's law
Hans Christian Ørsted discovers electromagnetism
Nicolas Léonard Sadi Carnot describes the Carnot cycle
Georg Ohm formulates Ohm's law
Friedrich Wöhler synthesizes urea
Michael Faraday discovers electromagnetic induction
Matthias Schleiden and Theodor Schwann formulate cell theory
Julius Robert von Mayer proposes the law of conservation of energy
Johann Gottfried Galle discovers Neptune
Gregor Mendel begins his experiments on plant hybridization
Charles Darwin publishes On the Origin of Species
Louis Pasteur disproves spontaneous generation
James Clerk Maxwell publishes A Dynamical Theory of the Electromagnetic Field
Pierre Janssen and Norman Lockyer discover helium
Dmitri Mendeleev publishes the periodic table
Johannes Diderik van der Waals derives the van der Waals equation
Ludwig Boltzmann formulates the statistical definition of entropy
Thomas Edison invents a practical incandescent light bulb
Robert Koch discovers the bacterium that causes tuberculosis
Louis Pasteur successfully tests the rabies vaccine
The Michelson–Morley experiment yields a null result for the aether
Heinrich Hertz discovers radio waves
Wilhelm Röntgen discovers X-rays
Henri Becquerel discovers radioactivity
J. J. Thomson discovers the electron
Marie and Pierre Curie discover polonium and radium
Max Planck formulates Planck's law of black-body radiation
Karl Landsteiner discovers human blood groups
Albert Einstein publishes his Annus Mirabilis papers
The Geiger-Marsden experiment is conducted
Ernest Rutherford proposes the planetary model of the atom
Alfred Wegener proposes continental drift
Niels Bohr proposes the Bohr model of the atom
Albert Einstein formulates general relativity
Arthur Eddington's eclipse expedition confirms general relativity
Alexander Friedmann predicts the expansion of the universe
Louis de Broglie proposes wave-particle duality
Wolfgang Pauli formulates the Pauli exclusion principle
Erwin Schrödinger publishes the Schrödinger equation
Werner Heisenberg formulates the uncertainty principle
Alexander Fleming discovers penicillin
Edwin Hubble discovers the expansion of the universe
Clyde Tombaugh discovers Pluto
James Chadwick discovers the neutron
Otto Hahn and Fritz Strassmann discover nuclear fission
Enrico Fermi oversees the first controlled nuclear chain reaction
Oswald Avery proves DNA is the genetic material
The transistor is invented at Bell Labs
George Gamow, Ralph Alpher, and Robert Herman propose the Big Bang theory
Rosalind Franklin captures Photograph 51 of DNA
The Hershey-Chase experiment confirms DNA is the genetic material
James Watson and Francis Crick propose the double helix structure of DNA
The Soviet Union launches Sputnik 1
Theodore Maiman builds the first working laser
Yuri Gagarin becomes the first human in space
Murray Gell-Mann and George Zweig propose the quark model
Arno Penzias and Robert Wilson discover the cosmic microwave background radiation
Jocelyn Bell Burnell discovers the first pulsar
Apollo 11 lands the first humans on the Moon
Stephen Hawking predicts Hawking radiation
The Voyager spacecraft are launched
Alan Guth proposes cosmic inflation
The Scanning Tunneling Microscope is invented
Kary Mullis invents the polymerase chain reaction
High-temperature superconductivity is discovered
The Human Genome Project begins
The first exoplanet around a main-sequence star, 51 Pegasi b, is discovered
Dolly the sheep becomes the first cloned mammal
The accelerated expansion of the universe is discovered
